metadata.name: ISO 28000:2022 Security and Resilience — Supply Chain Security Management Systems
metadata.description: ISO 28000:2022 specifies requirements for a security management system for the supply chain, enabling organizations to identify security threats and manage supply chain security risks. The 2022 revision modernized the standard to align with the High-Level Structure (HLS) used by ISO 9001, 14001, and 45001.
metadata.classification_reasoning: ISO 28000 is the international standard for supply chain security management systems, used by logistics providers, ports, customs operators, and organizations with complex global supply chains.
metadata.framework_purpose: To specify requirements for a security management system for supply chains, enabling organizations to effectively address security threats and implement appropriate security measures throughout the supply chain.
metadata.framework_scope: Organizations of all types and sizes involved in manufacturing, service, storage, or transportation of goods at any stage of the production or supply process.
framework_objectives: Identify and assess supply chain security threats and risks
Implement effective security measures across the supply chain
Ensure business continuity through supply chain security
Comply with customs and trade security requirements
Build supply chain resilience and stakeholder trust
metadata.target_audience: Supply Chain Managers, Logistics Managers, Security Officers, Trade Compliance Managers, Port Authorities, Freight Forwarders, and Customs Brokers
adoption_approach: Define supply chain security management system scope
Assess supply chain security risks and threats
Implement security controls at critical supply chain points
Train personnel on supply chain security

controls.title: Supply Chain Security Context
controls.description: The organization shall determine external and internal issues that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcomes of its supply chain security management system.
controls.full_text: Conduct analysis of the supply chain security context including: geographic regions of operation, modes of transport, types of goods, regulatory requirements (C-TPAT, AEO, customs requirements), threat landscape, and organizational security capabilities. This analysis informs the security risk assessment.
evidence_requirements: Geographic and transport mode risk analysis

controls.title: Supply Chain Security Risk Assessment
controls.description: The organization shall establish, implement, and maintain processes to identify, analyze, and assess supply chain security risks.
controls.full_text: Conduct comprehensive supply chain security risk assessment covering: physical security risks (theft, sabotage, unauthorized access), cyber security risks to logistics systems, personnel security risks, cargo integrity risks, and geopolitical risks. Assess risks along the entire supply chain from suppliers to customers.

controls.title: Security Objectives
controls.description: The organization shall establish security objectives at relevant functions, levels, and processes of the supply chain security management system.
controls.full_text: Set measurable supply chain security objectives aligned with the security policy. Objectives address identified risks and compliance requirements. Action plans define measures, responsible parties, timelines, and performance indicators for achieving objectives.

controls.title: Operational Planning and Control
controls.description: The organization shall plan, implement, control, and maintain the processes needed to meet supply chain security requirements.
controls.full_text: Implement operational controls for supply chain security covering: physical facility security (fencing, lighting, access control), cargo security (sealing, scanning, tracking), vehicle and conveyance security, personnel security (background checks, vetting), and technology security (cargo tracking systems).

controls.title: Physical Security
controls.description: The organization shall implement physical security measures to prevent unauthorized access to facilities, vehicles, and cargo.
controls.full_text: Implement physical security controls at all critical supply chain locations including warehouses, distribution centers, ports, and vehicles. Controls include perimeter security (fencing, gates, barriers), access control systems, CCTV surveillance, lighting, intrusion detection, and security personnel. Conduct regular security patrols and inspections.

controls.title: Cargo and Container Security
controls.description: The organization shall implement controls to ensure the integrity and security of cargo and containers throughout the supply chain.
controls.full_text: Implement cargo integrity controls including container sealing procedures (ISO 17712 high-security seals), seal inspection at key handover points, cargo count verification, seal number documentation, and anomaly reporting. Implement cargo tracking for high-value or high-risk shipments.

controls.title: Personnel and Access Security
controls.description: The organization shall implement personnel security measures including background checks, access control, and security awareness.
controls.full_text: Implement personnel security including pre-employment background screening, security clearances for sensitive roles, identity verification, access control based on roles, and visitor management. Train all personnel on supply chain security awareness. Implement processes for reporting suspicious activities.

controls.title: Cyber Security for Supply Chain
controls.description: The organization shall implement cyber security measures to protect information and technology systems supporting supply chain operations.
controls.full_text: Protect supply chain IT systems (ERP, TMS, WMS, tracking systems) from cyber threats. Implement access controls, network security, data encryption, and incident response for cyber events affecting supply chain operations. Manage cyber risks from suppliers and logistics partners.
evidence_requirements: Cyber incident response for supply chain

controls.title: Supply Chain Partner Security Requirements
controls.description: The organization shall establish security requirements for supply chain partners including suppliers, logistics providers, and subcontractors.
controls.full_text: Establish and communicate security requirements to all supply chain partners. Conduct security assessments of critical suppliers and logistics providers. Include security requirements in contracts. Monitor partner security performance. Consider partner security status in authorized economic operator (AEO) programs.

controls.title: Security Incident Response
controls.description: The organization shall establish, implement, and maintain processes to detect, respond to, and recover from supply chain security incidents.
controls.full_text: Develop and maintain supply chain security incident response plans for events such as cargo theft, tampering, unauthorized access, piracy, and cyber incidents affecting logistics. Define escalation procedures and notification requirements to customers, carriers, customs authorities, and law enforcement.

controls.title: Regulatory Compliance (C-TPAT/AEO)
controls.description: The organization shall maintain compliance with applicable supply chain security regulations and voluntary programs such as C-TPAT, AEO, and ISO 28000.
controls.full_text: Maintain compliance with supply chain security regulations including Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism (C-TPAT), Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) programs, ISPS Code (maritime), cargo security regulations, and border control requirements. Seek trusted trader status where beneficial.

controls.title: Security Performance Monitoring
controls.description: The organization shall monitor, measure, analyze, and evaluate security performance.
controls.full_text: Monitor supply chain security performance through KPIs including: cargo theft incidents, seal integrity failures, security breaches, access control violations, security training completion, and supplier security compliance. Analyze trends and investigate anomalies. Report performance to management.

controls.title: Internal Security Audit
controls.description: The organization shall conduct internal audits to provide information on whether the security management system conforms to requirements and is effectively implemented.
controls.full_text: Conduct internal security audits at all supply chain locations and for all key security processes. Auditors must be independent and competent. Audit findings are reported to management. Nonconformances require corrective action. Audit programs are risk-based and cover all high-risk supply chain nodes.

controls.title: Supply Chain Security Policy
controls.description: Top management shall establish a supply chain security policy appropriate to the organization and its security risk profile.
controls.full_text: Establish a supply chain security policy committing to: protection of goods and information throughout the supply chain, compliance with applicable security regulations, continual improvement of security performance, and security requirements for supply chain partners. Communicate the policy throughout the organization and to partners.

controls.title: Security Competence
controls.description: The organization shall ensure that persons involved in supply chain security are competent based on appropriate education, training, and experience.
controls.full_text: Define security competency requirements for all roles involved in supply chain security. Provide security training including initial training and refresher training. Conduct competency assessments for critical security roles. Maintain training records.

controls.title: Continual Improvement of Supply Chain Security
controls.description: The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the supply chain security management system.
controls.full_text: Implement continual improvement of supply chain security through systematic analysis of security incidents, near misses, audit findings, emerging threats, and best practices. Share security learnings across the supply chain. Improve security controls proactively based on threat intelligence.
